response.justification: The candidate is an experienced full-stack software developer with strong web, backend, and some systems programming background, but the role requires deep specialization in SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator performance analysis, and hardware-software co-design. The resume shows no experience with computer architecture, GPU/accelerator workloads, deep learning performance optimization, or silicon modeling. While the low-latency DLang work and C/C++ tooling experience demonstrate solid engineering fundamentals, the domain gap to a principal-level hardware performance architect role is too wide.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software engineering experience across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded systems
Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (millions of HTTP requests/sec with async I/O, fibers, caching)
Strong engineering craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, open-source authorship, and technical community teaching
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, silicon architecture, or hardware-software co-design experience
No AI/deep-learning workload, GPU architecture, or SIMD/MIMD performance analysis background
Career trajectory is web/product engineering, not hardware performance architecture at principal level

key_factors.factor: No AI accelerator or GPU workload background
Solid low-latency systems and performance engineering fundamentals
